summary: Current HEAD 3da7bb249c8ac1212f53f26bf06432fb1d7d6071 has release-relevant unit and integration coverage for the one-command PR-ready closure. The public CLI production-shaped E2E exercises managed-worktree implementation, verification, independent review, needs_changes repair, fresh review, and current-HEAD final prepare; owner and inherited Guarded Run tests cover the typed stop, persistence, resume, cancellation, timeout, quota, and failure matrix. No blocking unit/integration coverage gap was found.
inspection_summary: Inspected the role request, Story acceptance criteria, Story test plan, current strict-head verification evidence, expensive verification/e2e/integration/typecheck/build/conformance ledgers, the production-shaped public CLI E2E, one-command owner tests, Guarded Run persistence and authority tests, agent runtime adapter tests, and independent review orchestration tests. The latest regression change removes manually seeded implementation provenance and proves review execution is bound to the current run's managed worktree and authority.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the review request marked reusable verification stale after a unit-evidence update. Final judgment: the persisted verification-evidence latest unit, integration, and e2e records are all bound to the exact current HEAD and current user-input fingerprint, so the request-generation ordering does not make the reviewed test evidence stale.

Initial concern: the public E2E might bypass production action owners or rely on manually seeded provenance. Final judgment: S-001 invokes the public execute run CLI and production owner composition with real managed-worktree Git commits, while the latest Guarded Run regression removes the manual provenance seed and validates implementation and independent review through the current run authority.

Initial concern: coverage might prove only the happy path. Final judgment: owner, Guarded Run, runtime adapter, and review orchestrator tests collectively cover missing artifacts, exact human-decision descriptors, malformed decisions, verification failure, CI pending, no progress, quota, timeout, provider and parse failure, retry or asynchronous dispatch, cancellation and containment, restart and resume, stale-head rejection, review needs_changes repair, and convergence exhaustion.
